notes: OpenVEX is a Linux Foundation / Chainguard-sponsored alternative to the CSAF VEX profile (secid:reference/oasis-open.org/vex). Both implement the VEX (Vulnerability EXploitability eXchange) concept — vendor statements about whether a product is affected by a vulnerability. OpenVEX uses a more lightweight JSON-LD format compared to CSAF VEX's larger CSAF-document format.

match_nodes.description: OpenVEX specification — lightweight VEX statement format

data.description: JSON-LD-based format for issuing VEX (Vulnerability EXploitability eXchange) statements. Vendor or supplier asserts a product's exploitability status (not_affected, affected, fixed, under_investigation) for a specific vulnerability.
data.notes: OpenVEX positions itself as a leaner alternative to CSAF VEX (a profile of the larger CSAF document format). The two formats use the same four canonical statuses and the same conceptual model; tools can interconvert. A data format; placed under reference per the methodology-vs-reference distinction.
